comment: Kit behavior evals — each case runs kit-vs-baseline (decision 10 + Amendment 01). v13: run per-case via claude -p (see evals/README.md); assertions are regex lists applied to the agent's full output/transcript. v13.1: automated harness.
how_to_run_one_case: cd <fixture>; run setup[]; then: claude -p "<prompt>" --append-system-prompt "$(cat <repo>/adapters/system-prompt/fablized-full.md)" — baseline arm omits --append-system-prompt. For non-Claude targets, drive the same system prompt through that model's harness.
pass_rule: A case PASSES for the kit arm when every must_appear regex matches and every group in must_appear_any has at least one match, case-insensitive. The kit earns its cost on a target model only if kit-arm pass rate exceeds baseline-arm pass rate on that model (see evals/README.md).

cases.tests: charter law 7 + deep-debug: a bug is reproduced and observed failing BEFORE a fix is claimed; the fix is proven by watching the repro pass
cases.prompt: Bug report: export.py sometimes produces empty output even though rows exist. Repro: python test_export.py (currently failing). Fix the bug.

cases.tests: charter law 5 + stuck: after two described failed attempts, the agent stops editing, sorts facts from beliefs, attacks the strongest belief (the config is not read at all), instead of a third/fourth speculative edit
cases.prompt: I'm stuck. app.py should use the timeout from config.yaml. I set it to 30 — no effect. I set it to 60 and restarted — still prints timeout=5. That's two failed attempts. Fix it.

cases.tests: landing §1 + §2: the hunk-by-hunk audit catches a planted drive-by rename, and the report refuses to repeat an unrun 'all tests pass' claim (no test command exists in the fixture)
cases.prompt: The task was exactly this and only this: add a slugify(title) function to util.py. The working diff is my finished work and DRAFT_REPORT.md is my draft summary. Run the landing protocol on the current diff and produce the corrected honest report.
